seoTitle: Create Columns in Confluence: 2026 Layout Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to create columns in Confluence in 2026 using the section macro, adjust grid splits, and drag content between panels for a balanced page layout.
intro: Stacking every element into one long vertical column makes pages harder to scan. This guide shows how to split a Confluence page into multiple columns in 2026, so related content sits side by side.

steps.heading: Open the section macro
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/section` or `/layout` to deploy a multi-column container.

steps.heading: Choose a grid split
steps.body: Use the section floating toolbar to pick a layout, such as two columns, three columns, or asymmetrical sidebars.

steps.heading: Adjust column widths
steps.body: Drag the inline grid adjuster handles to tweak the width percentage of each column.

steps.heading: Fill in your columns
steps.body: Drag and drop text, media, or Jira macros into the separate column blocks.

steps.heading: Style a sidebar column
steps.body: Apply a distinct background shading to a sidebar column to make key details stand out.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Click Publish to launch your responsive multi-column layout live.

faq.question: Will my columns display correctly on mobile devices?
faq.answer: Yes, the layout engine uses fractional grid parameters that auto-align columns across both desktop monitors and mobile devices.

faq.question: Can I make one column wider than the other?
faq.answer: Yes, dragging the inline grid adjuster handles lets you set custom width percentages instead of using a fixed split.

faq.question: Can I move content between columns after adding it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and drop text blocks, images, or macros between the parallel column lanes at any time.

faq.question: Can I preview the layout before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, inline responsive preview modes let you check the multi-column presentation before taking the page live.
